briefdescription.para: Storage container for the characters.
Mutable forward iterator.
Immutable forward iterator.
Mutable reverse iterator.
Immutable reverse iterator.
Unsigned size type.
Signed distance type.
Stored value type.
Mutable element reference.
Immutable element reference.
Mutable element pointer.
Immutable element pointer.
No valid position.
Shared backing storage.
Visible sub-range inside _data.
Create an empty terminal string.
Create a terminal string from UTF-8 text.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 text to split into terminal characters.

How UTF-8 encoding errors are handled. Control codes are ignored except for tab and newline. Use EncodingErrors::Throw to make malformed UTF-8 explicit at the string-construction boundary. EncodingErrors::Replace replaces each malformed UTF-8 byte with one Unicode replacement character.
If encodingErrors is EncodingErrors::Throw and the text is not valid UTF-8, or if the text contains an unsupported character sequence.

briefdescription.para: Create a terminal string from UTF-8 text with a uniform style.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 text to split into terminal characters.
The style to use for the characters.

How UTF-8 encoding errors are handled. Control codes are ignored except for tab and newline. Use EncodingErrors::Throw to make malformed UTF-8 explicit at the string-construction boundary. EncodingErrors::Replace replaces each malformed UTF-8 byte with one Unicode replacement character.
If encodingErrors is EncodingErrors::Throw and the text is not valid UTF-8, or if the text contains an unsupported character sequence.

briefdescription.para: Create a terminal string from UTF-32 text.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 text to split into terminal characters. Control codes are ignored except for tab and newline.
If the text contains an unsupported character sequence.

briefdescription.para: Create a terminal string from UTF-32 text with a uniform style.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 text to split into terminal characters.
The style to use for the characters. Control codes are ignored except for tab and newline.
If the text contains an unsupported character sequence.

briefdescription.para: Create a terminal string repeating the same Char.
Create a terminal string by materializing one read-only view.

parameterdescription.para: The string view to copy into an owned string.

briefdescription.para: Compare two strings.

detaileddescription.para: Two strings are only equal, if all characters and styles are equal.

briefdescription.para: Access one character without bounds checking.

simplesect.para: A copy of the character at index, or Char{} if index is out of bounds.

briefdescription.para: Access one character without bounds checking.

simplesect.para: A mutable reference to the character at index, or a discarded Char{} if index is out of bounds.

briefdescription.para: Append one character to this string.
Append another terminal string to this string.
Append a read-only string view to this string.
Create a new string with one appended character.
Create a new string with another appended string.
Create a new string with an appended read-only view.
Get the number of stored characters.
Get the width of the string in terminal cells.

simplesect.para: The sum of all character display widths.

briefdescription.para: Test if this string is empty.
Access one character with bounds checking.

simplesect.para: A copy of the character at index.

briefdescription.para: Get an iterator to the first character.
Get an iterator past the last character.
Get a const iterator to the first character.
Get a const iterator past the last character.
Get a const iterator to the first character.
Get a const iterator past the last character.
Get a reverse iterator to the last character.
Get a reverse iterator past the first character.
Get a const reverse iterator to the last character.
Get a const reverse iterator past the first character.
Get a const reverse iterator to the last character.
Get a const reverse iterator past the first character.
Count the number of characters with a given color.

simplesect.para: The number of characters in this string.

briefdescription.para: Count the number of characters matching any color.

simplesect.para: The number of characters in this string.

briefdescription.para: Get the index of the next character with a given color.

detaileddescription.para:
If startIndex is out of bounds, returns npos. If no character is found, returns npos.
character
The character to search for. Compares both, character and color!
startIndex
The start index to search from. Defaults to 0.
The index of the next character or npos if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get the index of the next matching character.

detaileddescription.para:
Ignores the character style. If startIndex is out of bounds, returns npos. If no character is found, returns npos.
character
The character to search for. Only compares single-code-point characters.
startIndex
The start index to search from. Defaults to 0.
The index of the next character or npos if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get the index of the next matching character.

detaileddescription.para:
Ignores the character style. If startIndex is out of bounds, returns npos. If no character is found, returns npos. This function matches any character in the character set.
characterSet
The character-set to search for. Only compares single-code-point characters.
startIndex
The start index to search from. Defaults to 0.
The index of the next character or npos if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get the index of the next not matching character.

detaileddescription.para:
Ignores the character style. If startIndex is out of bounds, returns npos. If no character is found, returns npos. This function matches if no character in the character set matches the text.
characterSet
The character-set to search for. Only compares single-code-point characters.
startIndex
The start index to search from. Defaults to 0.
The index of the next character or npos if not found.

briefdescription.para: Get a substring.

detaileddescription.para:
If startIndex is out of bounds, returns an empty string. If length exceeds the string length, returns the remaining characters.
startIndex
The start index of the substring.
length
The number of characters after the start index.
The substring or an empty string if startIndex is out of bounds.

briefdescription.para: Get a substring that fits into the given display width.

detaileddescription.para:
If a double-sized character is at the edge, it isn't included in the result. Therefore, the resulting string may be shorter than the display width.
displayWidth
The maximum width of the substring in display units.
alignment
The alignment of the cropped text. Only Alignment::Left and Alignment::Right are supported.
The cropped substring or an empty string if displayWidth is <=0.

briefdescription.para: Return a string with the given characters trimmed from the beginning and end.

detaileddescription.para:
Only single-code-point characters are matched.
characters
The characters to remove from both ends. If empty, removes space, tab, and newline characters.
A copy without matching leading and trailing characters.

briefdescription.para: Return a normalized string.

detaileddescription.para:
Trim the given characters from start/end, and eplace any number of the given characters inside the string with a single separator. Only single-code-point characters are matched. The style of replaced characters is determined by using the first matches character style as a base for separator.
characters
The characters to match. If empty: space, tab, and newline characters.
separator
The separator to use.

briefdescription.para: Test if this string contains control characters.

detaileddescription.para: Most control codes are filtered on construction, therefore, this test searched for NL and TAB.

briefdescription.para: Reserve storage for at least the given number of characters.
Remove all characters from this string.
Trim the given characters from the beginning and end of the string.

detaileddescription.para:
Only single-code-point characters are matched.
characters
The characters to remove from both ends. If empty, removes space, tab, and newline characters.

briefdescription.para: Normalize this string.

detaileddescription.para:
Trim the given characters from start/end, and eplace any number of the given characters inside the string with a single separator. Only single-code-point characters are matched. The style of replaced characters is determined by using the first matches character style as a base for separator.
characters
The characters to match. If empty: space, tab, and newline characters.
separator
The separator to use.

briefdescription.para: Replace characters in this string.

detaileddescription.para:
If the range is out of bounds, it is clamped to the string's length. If the range is outside the string or empty, no replacement is performed. If the operation does not change the string, no replacement is performed.
range
The range of characters to replace.
replacement
The replacement for this range.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Remove a part of the string.

detaileddescription.para: If the range is out of bounds, it is clamped to the string's length. If the range is outside the string or empty, no replacement is performed.

briefdescription.para: Set a character in this string.

detaileddescription.para: This call is more efficient than using the index operator.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::size_t pos, const StringView &other, CharStyle style) noexcept
memberdef.name: insertWithBaseStyle

briefdescription.para: Append another terminal string with a base style.

detaileddescription.para:
If pos is out of range, appends the string at the end.
pos
The position to insert the string at.
other
The source text view.
style
The style used as base for inherited components in the appended range.

briefdescription.para: Append text using one uniform style.

parameterdescription.para: The style applied to the appended characters.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
Append another terminal string with a base style.

parameterdescription.para: The style used as base for inherited components in the appended range.

briefdescription.para: Append a repeated character.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::size_t count, char32_t character, CharStyle style) noexcept
memberdef.name: append

briefdescription.para: Append a repeated Unicode code point with a uniform style.

parameterdescription.para: The style for every appended character.

briefdescription.para: Append elements to this string.

detaileddescription.para:
This works similar to Terminal::print(). If you add a color, this color is "active" for all following characters in the same call. If you add character attributes, these attributes are active for all following characters in the same call. Appended Char, String, and StringView values with inherited color components or inherited attributes resolve against the currently active state. Just adding a color does not change the string.
args
The arguments to append.

briefdescription.para: Split the string into words at space, tab, carriage return, or newline characters.
Wrap this string into lines that have a maximum display width.

detaileddescription.para:
Paragraph breaks from newline characters are preserved using the selected paragraph spacing.
width
The maximum terminal width in cells. Must be greater than zero.
paragraphSpacing
The spacing to use between newline-separated paragraphs.
A sequence of lines.

briefdescription.para: Count how many terminal lines this string occupies for a given terminal width.

detaileddescription.para:
Newline characters start a new terminal line and printable characters wrap at the given width. The result is undefined for abstract terminal widths smaller than 10 cells.
width
The available terminal width in cells. Must be greater than zero.
The number of occupied terminal lines.

briefdescription.para: Get the natural rectangular size for this text without wrapping.

detaileddescription.para:
The returned size is at least 1x1, preserves explicit non-trailing newline characters as separate lines, and uses terminal cell width for wide and combining characters.
The natural text size in terminal cells.

briefdescription.para: Calculate the height required to render this text with WritableBuffer::drawText().

detaileddescription.para:
The given width is the full target rectangle width, including margins configured in options.
width
The available rectangle width in terminal cells.
options
The text options used for paragraph layout.
The required rectangle height in terminal cells.

briefdescription.para: Splits this string into individual lines.

detaileddescription.para:
The string is split at the NL character that is not included in the result. Empty lines are preserved. A NL at the end of the string does not generate an additional empty line.
A sequence of lines.

briefdescription.para: Create a new string with the given style applied as a base.

parameterdescription.para: The style used as base for the resulting string.

simplesect.para: A new string with the base style applied.

memberdef.argsstring: (const StringView &other, std::size_t startIndex, std::size_t length=npos) noexcept
memberdef.name: appendRange

memberdef.argsstring: (const StringView &other, std::size_t startIndex, std::size_t length, CharStyle style) noexcept
memberdef.name: appendRangeWithBaseStyle

memberdef.argsstring: (std::initializer_list< std::string_view > lines, Color color={}, CharAttributes attributes={}) noexcept
memberdef.name: fromLines

briefdescription.para: Create a new string from a list of lines.

detaileddescription.para:
All lines are joined using a new-line character.
lines
The lines for the string.
color
The base color to use for each character.
attributes
The base attributes to use for each character. Invalid UTF-8 bytes are replaced with the Unicode replacement character.
The new string.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::initializer_list< std::u32string_view > lines, Color color={}, CharAttributes attributes={}) noexcept -> String
memberdef.name: fromLines

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (std::string_view str, Color color={}, CharAttributes attributes={}, EncodingErrors encodingErrors=EncodingErrors::Replace) -> Storage
memberdef.name: splitCharacters

briefdescription.para: A terminal string represented as a sequence of Char values.

detaileddescription.para: String is implicitly shared. Copying a string shares its backing data, and a deep copy is only made when one instance is modified through a mutating API.
